message:
fix: Correct dep tree test to verify dependency traversal direction

The dep tree command was updated to show dependencies (what an issue
depends ON) rather than dependents (what depends on an issue). This
commit updates the reproduction test to match the corrected behavior.

Diamond dependency scenario:
    A
   / \
  B   C
   \ /
    D

Previous (incorrect) test:
- Started tree from D (the blocker)
- Expected to see B, C, and A as dependents
- This was testing "what depends on D" (reverse direction)

Corrected test:
- Starts tree from A (the root issue)
- Shows B and C as direct dependencies of A
- Shows D appearing twice (under both B and C)
- This tests "what does A depend on" (forward direction)

The assertion now correctly verifies that D (the common dependency)
appears twice in the tree output, once under each path through the
diamond (A->B->D and A->C->D).

Comments updated to clarify:
- "dep tree" walks dependencies (what issue depends on)
- Not dependents (what depends on issue)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
